items.title: Anthropic Raises $65B, Hits $965B Valuation Surpassing OpenAI
items.details: Anthropic closed a $65 billion Series H round at a $965 billion post-money valuation, co-led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, making it the world's most valuable private AI startup. Strategic infrastructure partners including Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron also joined, and $15 billion of the round came from previously committed hyperscaler investments including $5 billion from Amazon. The round came the same day Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8 and confidentially filed for an IPO.

items.title: Anthropic Confidentially Files for IPO at $965B Valuation
items.details: On June 2, 2026, Anthropic formally submitted a confidential draft IPO filing, potentially leapfrogging rival OpenAI to a Wall Street debut as early as fall 2026. The company's run-rate revenue crossed $47 billion in late May 2026, and the Wall Street Journal reported it expects a 130% revenue surge leading to its first operating profit. Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley are under consideration for key underwriting roles.

items.title: Anthropic $47B ARR Eclipses OpenAI's $25B in Revenue Race
items.details: Anthropic reported a $47 billion revenue run rate in late May 2026, up from $30 billion earlier in the year and just $10 billion in full-year 2025, driven primarily by the Claude Code AI coding assistant. OpenAI generated approximately $5.7 billion in Q1 2026 revenue alone, with enterprise now making up over 40% of its mix. Counterpoint Research data showed Anthropic leading the LLM market with a 31.4% revenue share versus OpenAI's 29.0%.

items.title: OpenAI Projects $85B Losses in 2028 Despite Record Revenue
items.details: Confidential financial documents published by the Wall Street Journal ahead of OpenAI's IPO revealed the company projects spending $121 billion on compute in 2028 alone, resulting in projected losses of $85 billion that year. OpenAI does not expect to break even until after 2030. By contrast, Anthropic's training costs peak at roughly $30 billion in the same period — about 4x less — with the company projecting profitability as early as 2028 or 2029.

items.title: Q1 2026 VC Hits Record $300B; AI Absorbs 80% of Global Funding
items.details: Crunchbase data showed investors poured $300 billion into 6,000 startups globally in Q1 2026, up over 150% quarter-over-quarter and year-over-year — an all-time record. AI accounted for $242 billion, or 80% of total global venture funding, up from 55% in Q1 2025. Four of the five largest venture rounds ever were closed in Q1: OpenAI ($122B), Anthropic ($30B), xAI ($20B), and Waymo ($16B), collectively representing 65% of all global VC.

items.title: OpenAI Targets Q4 2026 IPO at ~$1 Trillion Valuation
items.details: OpenAI is preparing to file its confidential IPO prospectus in the coming weeks, targeting a public debut in fall 2026 at a valuation approaching $1 trillion. The company previously closed its $122 billion funding round in March 2026 at an $852 billion post-money valuation. OpenAI's Stargate infrastructure initiative has grown to nearly 7 gigawatts of capacity with over $400 billion in planned investment.

items.title: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 Data Center Revenue Hits $75.2B, Up 92% YoY
items.details: NVIDIA's SEC filing showed Q1 FY2027 (ending April 2026) total revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year, with data center revenue alone reaching $75.2 billion — a 92% annual increase. The company holds approximately 80-90% of the AI accelerator market by revenue, with its Blackwell GPU architecture as the primary growth driver. NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang has stated the revenue opportunity for AI chips may reach at least $1 trillion through 2027.

items.title: Google Completes $32B Wiz Acquisition, Unifying AI-Cloud Security
items.details: Google LLC completed its $32 billion acquisition of cloud and AI security platform Wiz on March 11, 2026, after initial announcement in March 2025. Wiz joined Google Cloud while maintaining its brand and multi-cloud commitment, with Google committing $1.5 billion in combined cash and stock retention bonuses for Wiz's 1,800 employees. The combined platform aims to detect AI-generated threats, protect AI models, and enable unified security operations across all major cloud environments.

items.title: Alphabet Acquires Wiz; Palo Alto Buys CyberArk for $25B
items.details: In addition to the Wiz deal, Palo Alto Networks announced a $25 billion acquisition of CyberArk to accelerate identity security and AI integration, per PwC's 2026 Tech Deals Outlook. ServiceNow separately paid approximately $3 billion for Moveworks, acquiring its natural language AI platform to power enterprise workflow automation. AI-related M&A deals closed in Q1 2026 totaled 266, a 90% increase year-over-year according to CB Insights.

items.title: Shield AI Raises $2B at $12.7B Valuation for Defense Autonomy
items.details: Shield AI raised $1.5 billion in Series G equity at a $12.7 billion post-money valuation on March 26, 2026, led by Advent International and JPMorganChase's Security and Resiliency Initiative, with Blackstone committing $500 million in preferred equity financing. The San Diego-based company is projecting more than 80% revenue growth in 2026, equating to at least $540 million, and is using proceeds to acquire flight simulation company Aechelon Technology. Shield AI's Hivemind platform has been selected by the U.S. Air Force as a mission autonomy provider for its Collaborative Combat Aircraft program.

items.title: ElevenLabs Hits $500M ARR, Raises $500M at $11B Valuation
items.details: AI voice startup ElevenLabs raised $500 million at an $11 billion valuation in February 2026, a round led by Sequoia Capital with participation from Andreessen Horowitz, Lightspeed, and Iconiq, more than tripling its prior $3.3 billion valuation. The company subsequently surpassed $500 million in annual recurring revenue with institutional investors BlackRock and NVIDIA among new backers. Enterprise customers including Deutsche Telekom, Meta, Revolut, and Salesforce are driving adoption.

items.title: Legora Legal AI Raises $550M Series D at $5.55B Valuation
items.details: Legora, a collaborative AI platform for legal teams, raised $550 million in a Series D round at a $5.55 billion valuation, with investors including Insight Partners, Index Ventures, IVP, and Bessemer Venture Partners. The round came as Legora aggressively expanded into the U.S. market one year after initially entering it, bringing total funding to nearly $1 billion. The deal positions Legora alongside Harvey and Steno as one of the defining companies in the legal AI space.

items.title: White House Releases National AI Legislative Framework, Targets State Preemption
items.details: On March 20, 2026, the Trump Administration released a comprehensive National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence, urging Congress to establish a unified federal standard and preempt state AI laws deemed 'unduly burdensome.' The framework calls against creating any new federal AI rulemaking body, instead relying on existing sector-specific regulators and industry-led standards. An AI Litigation Task Force was established by the Attorney General in January 2026 to challenge conflicting state measures.

items.title: Colorado AI Act Enforcement Begins June 30; EU AI Act Under Revision
items.details: Colorado's landmark AI Act — one of the first comprehensive state AI governance laws targeting high-risk AI in consequential decisions — reaches enforcement on June 30, 2026, after being delayed from its original February 1 date following industry pushback. A March 2026 working group draft would repeal and reenact the law with a reset effective date of January 1, 2027. Separately, the EU's 'Digital Omnibus' proposal, advancing through the EU legislative process, seeks to delay application of certain high-risk AI requirements and revise the EU AI Act.

items.title: Enterprise GenAI Adoption Doubles to 65%; Global AI Spend Tops $300B
items.details: McKinsey's 2026 Global AI Survey found generative AI enterprise adoption doubled from 33% in 2024 to 65% in 2026, while 72% of enterprises now have at least one AI workload in production. Global spending on AI systems surpassed $300 billion in 2026 according to IDC, with the average enterprise AI budget of $3.7 million expected to grow 30%+ in 2027. The median time to ROI for enterprise AI deployments has dropped from 24 months in 2024 to 14 months.

items.title: 97% of Executives Deploy AI Agents; Only 29% See Significant ROI
items.details: Writer's 2026 Enterprise AI Adoption Survey (2,400 respondents) found nearly all executives (97%) report deploying AI agents in the past year, with 52% of employees already using them, and 80%+ of Fortune 500 running agents in production. Average enterprise AI spend is projected to jump 65% to $11.6 million in 2026 from roughly $7 million in 2025. Yet only 29% of executives report significant organizational ROI, and 54% of C-suite leaders admit AI adoption is 'tearing their company apart.'

items.title: AI Global Market Valued at ~$514B in 2026; Set to Hit $1T by 2029
items.details: The global AI market is valued at approximately $514.5 billion as of 2026 and is projected to cross the $1 trillion mark by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 30.6%, per multiple analyst estimates. Fortune Business Insights projects growth from $375.93 billion in 2026 to $2.48 trillion by 2034 at a 26.6% CAGR, with cloud deployments accounting for 71.6% of the market. Statista separately forecasts the total AI market worldwide will reach $617.62 billion by end of 2026, with the U.S. generating $414.89 billion of that.

items.title: Agentic AI Market Valued at $9.1B in 2026, to Hit $139B by 2034
items.details: Fortune Business Insights valued the global agentic AI market at $9.14 billion in 2026, projecting growth to $139.19 billion by 2034 at a 40.5% CAGR. North America dominates with a 33.6% market share, and telecom leads enterprise agentic AI deployment at 48%, followed by retail and CPG at 47%. Microsoft, IBM, NVIDIA, and Anthropic are identified as the key players driving the space.

items.title: Goldman Sachs Warns Hyperscaler Capex to Hit $527B in 2026; ROI Gap Widens
items.details: Goldman Sachs estimated Wall Street consensus for 2026 hyperscaler capital spending at $527 billion, with cumulative 2025–2027 AI capex projected at $1.15 trillion — more than double the $477 billion spent from 2022 through 2024. Goldman warned that justifying this capex would require an annual AI profit run-rate exceeding $1 trillion, more than double the $450 billion 2026 consensus income estimate. Global corporate AI investment reached $581.7 billion in 2025, up 130% year-over-year according to Stanford HAI's 2026 AI Index.

items.title: Anthropic Beats OpenAI in Business Adoption; Claude Code Captures 54% of AI Coding Market
items.details: Ramp's AI Index showed that, for the first time, more businesses are using Anthropic's AI (34.4%) than OpenAI's (32.3%) as of May 2026, with Anthropic's business adoption quadrupling over the past year while OpenAI's rose just 0.3%. Claude Code generated over $2.5 billion in annualized recurring revenue by early 2026, capturing approximately 54% of the AI coding assistant market. Anthropic now serves over 1,000 enterprise customers spending more than $1 million per year, with eight of the Fortune 10 as Claude customers.
